---
title: "HERA_descriptives"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    fig_caption: true
    code_folding: show
    self_contained: true
---


```{r}
#Source R File with Setup, Package Loading, and Output Folder Creation

source("HERA_00_setup.R")

# Create subfolder for descriptives outputs
descriptives_folder <- file.path(output_folder, "02_descriptives")
if (!dir.exists(descriptives_folder)) {
  dir.create(descriptives_folder, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("Created descriptives subfolder:", descriptives_folder, "\n")
}

# LOAD LATEST CLEAN DATA FILE 

# Load the dataset
df <- readRDS('/Users/toryeisenlohr-moul/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/HERA/03_analytic_projects/HERA-Primary ms/02_datasets_output/HERA_20260319_clean.rds')


```


# Import Data
```{r}
# --- 1. Setup ---

#"numdrinkstoday",

# Add all your continuous outcome variable names here
continuous_vars <- c("drsp_1", "drsp_2", "drsp_3", "drsp_4", "drsp_5", "drsp_6", "drsp_7", "drsp_8", 
               "drsp_9", "drsp_10", "drsp_11", "drsp_12", "drsp_13", "drsp_14",
               "drsp_15", "drsp_16", "drsp_17", "drsp_18", "drsp_19",
               "drsp_20", "drsp_21",
   "drsp_total", "drsp_distress", "drsp_labilsocsx", "drsp_breastpain", "drsp_eatsx", "drsp_lowarousal"
)

outcome_labels <- c(
  # --- Substance Use & Craving ---
  #"numdrinkstoday" = "Number of Drinks",

  # --- Mood & Affect (DRSP/PMDD) ---
  "drsp_1"              = "Depression",
  "drsp_2"         = "Hopelessness",
  "drsp_3"        = "Worthlessness",
  "drsp_4"          = "Anxiety",
  "drsp_5"       = "Mood Swings",
  "drsp_6"         = "Rejection Sensitivity",
  "drsp_7"            = "Anger/Irritability",
  "drsp_8"        = "Interpersonal Conflicts",
  "drsp_9"    = "Loss of Interest",
  "drsp_16"        = "Feeling Overwhelmed",
  "drsp_17"     = "Feeling Out of Control",

  # --- DRSP Aggregate Scores ---
  "drsp_total"       = "DRSP Total",
  "drsp_distress"    = "Distress (DRSP)",
  "drsp_labilsocsx"  = "Social/Affect Lability (DRSP)",
  "drsp_breastpain"  = "Breast Sx (DRSP)",
  "drsp_eatsx"       = "Eating Sx",
  "drsp_lowarousal"  = "Low Arousal Sx (DRSP)",

  # --- Physical & Cognitive Symptoms ---
  "drsp_10"        = "Difficulty Concentrating",
  "drsp_11"            = "Fatigue/Lethargy",
  "drsp_14"       = "Hypersomnia",
  "drsp_15"       = "Insomnia",
  "drsp_12"         = "Overeating",
  "drsp_13"     = "Specific Food Cravings",
  "drsp_18"      = "Breast Tenderness",
  "drsp_19"     = "Breast Swelling",
  "drsp_20"         = "Headache",
  "drsp_21"          = "Joint/Muscle Pain"
)
```

# ------ SMM ELIGIBILITY at 5 OBS PER PHASE - MORNING/ALCOHOL OUTCOMES ------
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr) # For pivot_longer in the combined plot

# 1. DATA PREP ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save smm eligibility data
alcsmmelig <- cycledata_check(df, "numdrinkstoday")

# Merge back to df by id
df <- df %>%
  left_join(alcsmmelig$by_id %>% select(id, numdrinkstoday_follicular, numdrinkstoday_luteal), by = "id")

# Create a clean plotting dataset with an explicit "Low Data" flag
# This makes the ggplot logic much simpler
plot_data <- alcsmmelig$by_id %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a boolean flag: TRUE if EITHER phase is < 5
    low_data_flag = numdrinkstoday_follicular < 5 | numdrinkstoday_luteal < 5,
    # Convert to factor for nice legend labels
    status = ifelse(low_data_flag, "Excluded (<5 days)", "Included")
  )

# 2. INDIVIDUAL PLOTS -----------------------------------------------------

# Follicular Plot
p_drink_follicular <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = id, y = numdrinkstoday_follicular, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<5 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 5, label = "5-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Follicular Phase Drinking Days", y = "Days", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drink_follicular)

# Luteal Plot (Fixed the object name to p_drink_luteal)
p_drink_luteal <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = id, y = numdrinkstoday_luteal, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<5 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 5, label = "5-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Luteal Phase Drinking Days", y = "Days", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drink_luteal)


# 3. COMBINED PLOT --------------------------------------------------------

# To show both phases side-by-side per ID, we pivot to long format
p_combined_drink <- plot_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(numdrinkstoday_follicular, numdrinkstoday_luteal), 
               names_to = "phase", 
               values_to = "days") %>%
  mutate(phase = recode(phase, "numdrinkstoday_follicular" = "Follicular", "numdrinkstoday_luteal" = "Luteal")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = id, y = days, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7) + # 'dodge' puts bars side-by-side
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<5 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  labs(title = "Drinking Days by Phase (Red = <5 days in EITHER phase)", 
       y = "Number of Drinking Days", 
       x = "Participant ID") +
  facet_wrap(~phase, ncol = 1) + # Optional: Split panels if you have many IDs
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

print(p_combined_drink)

save_png(plot = p_combined_drink,
         filename = "combined_drink_data_availability.png",
         folder = descriptives_folder,
         width = 10,
         height = 8,
         dpi = 300)


# 4. COUNTS ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Count number of ids where either follicular or luteal is <5
# We can use the flag we created earlier
num_ids_below_threshold <- sum(plot_data$low_data_flag)
cat("Number of IDs with fewer than 5 drinking days in either phase:", num_ids_below_threshold, "\n")

# Add number that do have >=5 in both phases
num_ids_above_threshold <- sum(!plot_data$low_data_flag)
cat("Number of IDs with 5 or more drinking days in both phases:", num_ids_above_threshold, "\n")
```

# ------ SMM ELIGIBILITY at 5 OBS PER PHASE - EVENING/DRSP OUTCOMES ------

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# 1. DATA PREP ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save smm eligibility data for 'drsp_1'
drsp_1smmelig <- cycledata_check(df, "drsp_1")

# Merge back to df by id
df <- df %>%
  left_join(drsp_1smmelig$by_id %>% select(id, drsp_1_follicular, drsp_1_luteal), by = "id")

# Create a clean plotting dataset with an explicit "Low Data" flag
plot_data <- drsp_1smmelig$by_id %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a boolean flag: TRUE if EITHER phase is < 5
    low_data_flag = drsp_1_follicular < 5 | drsp_1_luteal < 5,
    # Convert to factor for nice legend labels
    status = ifelse(low_data_flag, "Excluded (<5 days)", "Included")
  )

# 2. INDIVIDUAL PLOTS -----------------------------------------------------

# Follicular Plot
p_drsp_1_follicular <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = id, y = drsp_1_follicular, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<5 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 5, label = "5-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Follicular Phase Data (drsp_1)", y = "Days Present", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drsp_1_follicular)

# Luteal Plot
p_drsp_1_luteal <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = id, y = drsp_1_luteal, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<5 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 5, label = "5-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Luteal Phase Data (drsp_1)", y = "Days Present", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drsp_1_luteal)


# 3. COMBINED PLOT --------------------------------------------------------

# Pivot to long format to show both phases side-by-side
p_combined_drsp_1 <- plot_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(drsp_1_follicular, drsp_1_luteal), 
               names_to = "phase", 
               values_to = "days") %>%
  mutate(phase = recode(phase, "drsp_1_follicular" = "Follicular", "drsp_1_luteal" = "Luteal")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = id, y = days, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<5 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  labs(title = "Data Availability by Phase (Red = <5 days in EITHER phase)", 
       y = "Number of Days with Data", 
       x = "Participant ID") +
  facet_wrap(~phase, ncol = 1) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

print(p_combined_drsp_1)

save_png(plot = p_combined_drsp_1,
         filename = "combined_drsp_1_data_availability.png",
         folder = descriptives_folder,
         width = 10,
         height = 8,
         dpi = 300)

# 4. COUNTS ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Count number of ids where either follicular or luteal is <5
num_ids_below_threshold <- sum(plot_data$low_data_flag)
cat("Number of IDs with fewer than 5 days of 'drsp_1' data in either phase:", num_ids_below_threshold, "\n")

# Add number that do have >=5 in both phases
num_ids_above_threshold <- sum(!plot_data$low_data_flag)
cat("Number of IDs with 5 or more 'drsp_1' days in both phases:", num_ids_above_threshold, "\n")
```

# ------ OVERLAP IN AM/PM ELIGBILITY at 5 OBS PER PHASE ------

```{r}
# Create a venn diagram of (1) those with >=5 observations in both phases for numdrinkstoday, (2) those with >=5 observations in both phases for drsp_1 


library(ggvenn)
# Step 1: Identify ids with >=5 non-missing observations for each variable in both phases
ids_with_numdrinkstoday <- alcsmmelig$by_id %>%
  filter(numdrinkstoday_follicular >= 5 & numdrinkstoday_luteal >= 5) %>%
  pull(id)

ids_with_drsp_1 <- drsp_1smmelig$by_id %>%
  filter(drsp_1_follicular >= 5 & drsp_1_luteal >= 5) %>%
  pull(id)

# Step 2: Create a list of sets for the Venn diagram
sets <- list(
  DrinkToday = ids_with_numdrinkstoday,
  drsp_1 = ids_with_drsp_1
)

# Generate the Venn diagram
venn_plot <- ggvenn(
  sets, 
  show_percentage = FALSE,   # Show percentages in each intersection
  fill_color = c("#E69F00", "pink"),  # Colors for each circle
  stroke_size = 0.8,        # Border thickness
  set_name_size = 20,        # Set names size (Surveys, Hormones, CycleData)
  text_size = 20            # Size of numbers/percentages in intersections
) +
  ggtitle("Overlap of IDs with >=5 Observations in Both Phases for:") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 50, hjust = 0.5),  # Larger, centered title
    text = element_text(size = 20)                      # General text size adjustments
  )

# Display the plot
print(venn_plot)
# Save the plot with high resolution
save_png(plot = venn_plot, 
         filename = "venn_diagram_drink_drsp_1.png",
         folder = descriptives_folder,
         height = 8)  # Keep taller for Venn diagram



```




```{r}
# ------ SMM ELIGIBILITY at 4 OBS PER PHASE - MORNING/ALCOHOL OUTCOMES ------
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr) 

# 1. DATA PREP ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save smm eligibility data (saved as unique object '_4')
alcsmmelig_4 <- cycledata_check(df, "numdrinkstoday")

# Merge back to df by id - ensuring we don't overwrite main df columns if running multiple times
# (We select specific columns to avoid duplication issues if you run this repeatedly)
df <- df %>%
  left_join(alcsmmelig_4$by_id %>% select(id, numdrinkstoday_follicular, numdrinkstoday_luteal), by = "id", suffix = c("", "_4obs"))

# Create a clean plotting dataset 
plot_data_4 <- alcsmmelig_4$by_id %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a boolean flag: TRUE if EITHER phase is < 4
    low_data_flag = numdrinkstoday_follicular < 4 | numdrinkstoday_luteal < 4,
    status = ifelse(low_data_flag, "Excluded (<4 days)", "Included")
  )

# 2. INDIVIDUAL PLOTS -----------------------------------------------------

# Follicular Plot (Object renamed to end in _4)
p_drink_follicular_4 <- ggplot(plot_data_4, aes(x = id, y = numdrinkstoday_follicular, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<4 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 4, label = "4-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Follicular Phase Drinking Days (4 Obs Threshold)", y = "Days", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drink_follicular_4)

# Luteal Plot
p_drink_luteal_4 <- ggplot(plot_data_4, aes(x = id, y = numdrinkstoday_luteal, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<4 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 4, label = "4-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Luteal Phase Drinking Days (4 Obs Threshold)", y = "Days", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drink_luteal_4)


# 3. COMBINED PLOT --------------------------------------------------------

p_combined_drink_4 <- plot_data_4 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(numdrinkstoday_follicular, numdrinkstoday_luteal), 
               names_to = "phase", 
               values_to = "days") %>%
  mutate(phase = recode(phase, "numdrinkstoday_follicular" = "Follicular", "numdrinkstoday_luteal" = "Luteal")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = id, y = days, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<4 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  labs(title = "Drinking Days by Phase (Red = <4 days in EITHER phase)", 
       y = "Number of Drinking Days", 
       x = "Participant ID") +
  facet_wrap(~phase, ncol = 1) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

print(p_combined_drink_4)

# SAVE WITH UNIQUE FILENAME
save_png(plot = p_combined_drink_4,
         filename = "combined_drink_data_availability_4obs.png", 
         folder = descriptives_folder,
         width = 10,
         height = 8,
         dpi = 300)


# 4. COUNTS ---------------------------------------------------------------

num_ids_below_threshold_4 <- sum(plot_data_4$low_data_flag)
cat("IDs with fewer than 4 drinking days in either phase:", num_ids_below_threshold_4, "\n")

num_ids_above_threshold_4 <- sum(!plot_data_4$low_data_flag)
cat("IDs with 4 or more drinking days in both phases:", num_ids_above_threshold_4, "\n")
```


```{r}
# ------ SMM ELIGIBILITY at 4 OBS PER PHASE - EVENING/DRSP OUTCOMES ------

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# 1. DATA PREP ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save smm eligibility data unique object
drsp_1smmelig_4 <- cycledata_check(df, "drsp_1")

# Merge back
df <- df %>%
  left_join(drsp_1smmelig_4$by_id %>% select(id, drsp_1_follicular, drsp_1_luteal), by = "id", suffix = c("", "_4obs"))

# Create plotting dataset
plot_data_drsp_1_4 <- drsp_1smmelig_4$by_id %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a boolean flag: TRUE if EITHER phase is < 4
    low_data_flag = drsp_1_follicular < 4 | drsp_1_luteal < 4,
    status = ifelse(low_data_flag, "Excluded (<4 days)", "Included")
  )

# 2. INDIVIDUAL PLOTS -----------------------------------------------------

# Follicular Plot
p_drsp_1_follicular_4 <- ggplot(plot_data_drsp_1_4, aes(x = id, y = drsp_1_follicular, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<4 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 4, label = "4-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Follicular Phase Data (drsp_1) - 4 Obs", y = "Days Present", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drsp_1_follicular_4)

# Luteal Plot
p_drsp_1_luteal_4 <- ggplot(plot_data_drsp_1_4, aes(x = id, y = drsp_1_luteal, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<4 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = 4, label = "4-day threshold", hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5, color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Luteal Phase Data (drsp_1) - 4 Obs", y = "Days Present", x = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_drsp_1_luteal_4)


# 3. COMBINED PLOT --------------------------------------------------------

p_combined_drsp_1_4 <- plot_data_drsp_1_4 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(drsp_1_follicular, drsp_1_luteal), 
               names_to = "phase", 
               values_to = "days") %>%
  mutate(phase = recode(phase, "drsp_1_follicular" = "Follicular", "drsp_1_luteal" = "Luteal")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = id, y = days, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Excluded (<4 days)" = "red", "Included" = "lightblue")) +
  labs(title = "Data Availability by Phase (Red = <4 days in EITHER phase)", 
       y = "Number of Days with Data", 
       x = "Participant ID") +
  facet_wrap(~phase, ncol = 1) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

print(p_combined_drsp_1_4)

# SAVE WITH UNIQUE FILENAME
save_png(plot = p_combined_drsp_1_4,
         filename = "combined_drsp_1_data_availability_4obs.png",
         folder = descriptives_folder,
         width = 10,
         height = 8,
         dpi = 300)

# 4. COUNTS ---------------------------------------------------------------

num_ids_below_drsp_1_4 <- sum(plot_data_drsp_1_4$low_data_flag)
cat("IDs with fewer than 4 days of 'drsp_1' data in either phase:", num_ids_below_drsp_1_4, "\n")

num_ids_above_drsp_1_4 <- sum(!plot_data_drsp_1_4$low_data_flag)
cat("IDs with 4 or more 'drsp_1' days in both phases:", num_ids_above_drsp_1_4, "\n")
```


```{r}
# ------ OVERLAP IN AM/PM ELIGBILITY at 4 OBS PER PHASE ------

library(ggvenn)

# Step 1: Use the new unique objects (_4) to filter
ids_with_numdrinkstoday_4 <- alcsmmelig_4$by_id %>%
  filter(numdrinkstoday_follicular >= 4 & numdrinkstoday_luteal >= 4) %>%
  pull(id)

ids_with_drsp_1_4 <- drsp_1smmelig_4$by_id %>%
  filter(drsp_1_follicular >= 4 & drsp_1_luteal >= 4) %>%
  pull(id)

# Step 2: Create a list of sets
sets_4 <- list(
  DrinkToday = ids_with_numdrinkstoday_4,
  drsp_1 = ids_with_drsp_1_4
)

# Generate the Venn diagram
venn_plot_4 <- ggvenn(
  sets_4, 
  show_percentage = FALSE, 
  fill_color = c("#E69F00", "pink"), 
  stroke_size = 0.8,         
  set_name_size = 20,         
  text_size = 20             
) +
  ggtitle("Overlap of IDs with >=4 Observations in Both Phases") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 40, hjust = 0.5), # Slightly adjusted title size
    text = element_text(size = 20)                      
  )

print(venn_plot_4)

# SAVE WITH UNIQUE FILENAME
save_png(plot = venn_plot_4, 
         filename = "venn_diagram_drink_drsp_1_4obs.png",
         folder = descriptives_folder,
         height = 8)
```






```{r}
# Source the function and create variables

# Variables needed for this plotting chunk - only use variables that exist
drsp_vars <- c(
  "drsp_total", "drsp_distress", "drsp_labilsocsx", "drsp_breastpain", "drsp_eatsx", "drsp_lowarousal"
)

daily_items_raw <- c(
 "numdrinkstoday", "drsp_total", "drsp_distress", "drsp_eatsx", "drsp_breastpain", "drsp_lowarousal")

# Combine and filter to only existing variables
alldailyvars_desired <- unique(c(daily_items_raw, drsp_vars))
alldailyvars <- intersect(alldailyvars_desired, names(df))


```



```{r}
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Violin Plots Loop
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------


# 2. Loop through the variables
# (Assumes 'continuous_vars' is already defined from your previous scripts)
for (outcome_name in continuous_vars) {
  
  message(paste0("Plotting violin for: ", outcome_name))
  
  # Create the plot using .data[[outcome_name]] for dynamic selection
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = "", y = .data[[outcome_name]])) +
    geom_violin(fill = "lightblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, position = position_dodge(0.9), outlier.color = "red", outlier.size = 2) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Violin Plot of ", outcome_name),
      y = outcome_name,
      x = ""
    ) +
    theme_light(base_size = 14)
  
  # 3. Save the plot
  save_png(
    plot = p,
    filename = paste0("violin_", outcome_name, ".png"),
    folder = descriptives_folder
  )
  
  # Create a histogram for each and save that too
  p_hist <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[outcome_name]])) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "lightblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Histogram of ", outcome_name),
      x = outcome_name,
      y = "Count"
    ) +
    theme_light(base_size = 14)
  save_png(
    plot = p_hist,
    filename = paste0("histogram_", outcome_name, ".png"),
    folder = descriptives_folder
  )
}

```




# ICCs, Between- and Within-Person Associations of Study Variables
```{r f1_corr, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ===================================================================
# 1. Load Required Libraries
# ===================================================================
library(tidyverse)
library(rmcorr)
library(performance)
library(lme4)
library(ggplot2)

# ===================================================================
# 2. Define the Heatmap Generation Function (Final Version)
# ===================================================================
generate_correlation_heatmap <- function(
    data, daily_items_raw, id_var, final_labels = NULL, # <-- New argument
    sig.level = 0.05,
    highlight_diag = TRUE,
    show_legend = TRUE,
    label_size = 4.6,
    diag_label_size = 6,
    subtitle_text = "Upper: Within-person (rmcorr); Lower: Between-person (Spearman); Diagonal: ICC"
) {
  # (The first part of the function is the same as the last version)
  n_vars <- length(daily_items_raw)
  results_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_vars, ncol = n_vars,
                           dimnames = list(daily_items_raw, daily_items_raw))
  pval_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_vars, ncol = n_vars,
                        dimnames = list(daily_items_raw, daily_items_raw))
  
  summary_expressions <- purrr::map(daily_items_raw, ~ rlang::quo(mean(!!rlang::sym(.x), na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
    rlang::set_names(daily_items_raw)
    
  person_means <- data %>%
    group_by(!!rlang::sym(id_var)) %>%
    summarise(!!!summary_expressions, .groups = "drop")

  between_corr <- suppressWarnings(
    cor(person_means[, daily_items_raw], method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  )

  for (i in 1:(n_vars - 1)) for (j in (i + 1):n_vars) {
    x <- person_means[[daily_items_raw[i]]]
    y <- person_means[[daily_items_raw[j]]]
    if (sum(!is.na(x) & !is.na(y)) > 2) {
      test <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman"))
      results_matrix[i, j] <- between_corr[i, j]
      pval_matrix[i, j] <- test$p.value
    }
  }

  for (i in 1:n_vars) {
    var <- daily_items_raw[i]
    fml <- as.formula(paste0("`", var, "` ~ 1 + (1|`", id_var, "`)"))
    if (sum(!is.na(data[[var]])) > 1) {
      model_fit <- try(lmer(fml, data = data), silent = TRUE)
      if (!inherits(model_fit, "try-error")) {
        icc_val <- performance::icc(model_fit)$ICC_adjusted
        results_matrix[i, i] <- icc_val
        pval_matrix[i, i] <- 0
      }
    }
  }

  for (i in 2:n_vars) for (j in 1:(i - 1)) {
    var1 <- daily_items_raw[i]; var2 <- daily_items_raw[j]
    rmcorr_data <- data %>% select(all_of(c(id_var, var1, var2))) %>% drop_na()
    if (nrow(rmcorr_data) > 2) {
      rmcorr_result <- rmcorr::rmcorr(participant = id_var, measure1 = var1, measure2 = var2, dataset = rmcorr_data)
      results_matrix[i, j] <- rmcorr_result$r
      pval_matrix[i, j] <- rmcorr_result$p
    }
  }

  within_corr_matrix <- results_matrix
  within_corr_matrix[upper.tri(within_corr_matrix, diag = TRUE)] <- 0
  dist_matrix <- as.dist(1 - abs(within_corr_matrix))
  hclust_obj <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "ward.D2")
  corr_order <- rownames(within_corr_matrix)[hclust_obj$order]

  results_matrix_ordered <- results_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]
  pval_matrix_ordered    <- pval_matrix[corr_order, corr_order]

  plot_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(results_matrix_ordered))
  names(plot_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "value")
  plot_df$p.value <- as.vector(pval_matrix_ordered)
  plot_df$Var1 <- factor(plot_df$Var1, levels = rev(corr_order))
  plot_df$Var2 <- factor(plot_df$Var2, levels = corr_order)

  no_leading_zero <- function(x) {
    ifelse(is.na(x), NA_character_, sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", sprintf("%.2f", x)))
  }

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      is_diag = as.character(Var1) == as.character(Var2),
      is_sig  = p.value < sig.level,
      label   = no_leading_zero(value)
    )

  diag_df    <- plot_df %>% filter(is_diag)
  sig_df     <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & is_sig)
  nonsig_df  <- plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag & !is_sig)

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_tile(data = plot_df %>% filter(!is_diag),
              aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, fill = value), color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_tile(data = diag_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
              fill = "gray92", color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#6D9EC1", mid = "white", high = "#E46726",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "Correlation") +
    labs(title = "Multilevel Correlation Matrix of Daily Outcomes", subtitle = subtitle_text) +
    theme_light(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 20),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "italic", size = 12, margin = margin(b = 6)),
      axis.text.x   = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y   = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title    = element_blank(),
      legend.position = if (show_legend) "right" else "none",
      panel.grid.major = element_blank()
    )

  p <- p +
    geom_text(data = sig_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
              fontface = "bold", color = "black", size = label_size, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_text(data = nonsig_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
              color = "gray40", size = label_size, na.rm = TRUE)

  if (highlight_diag && nrow(diag_df) > 0) {
    p <- p +
      geom_text(data = diag_df, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1, label = label),
                fontface = "bold", color = "#4B0082", size = diag_label_size, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  # === NEW CODE TO RELABEL AXES AT THE END ===
  if (!is.null(final_labels)) {
    p <- p +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = final_labels) +
      scale_y_discrete(labels = final_labels)
  }
  # ===========================================

  return(p)
}

# ===================================================================
# 3. Define Parameters and Run the Plot
# ===================================================================
# Technical names mapped to the desired "pretty" labels
corrplotlist <- c(
 # --- Substance Use & Craving ---
#  "numdrinkstoday" = "Number of Drinks",

  # --- DRSP Aggregate Scores ---
  "drsp_total"       = "DRSP Total",
  "drsp_distress"    = "Distress (DRSP)",
  "drsp_labilsocsx"  = "Social/Affect Lability (DRSP)",
  "drsp_breastpain"  = "Breast Sx (DRSP)",
  "drsp_eatsx"       = "Eating Sx",
  "drsp_lowarousal"  = "Low Arousal Sx (DRSP)",
  "drsp_21"          = "Joint/Muscle Pain"
)

# This assumes `df` exists in your environment
if (exists("df") && exists("corrplotlist")) {

  # === NEW: Create safe labels for calculation and a lookup table for plotting ===
  # 1. Create safe labels (e.g., "Hyperactive.Impulsive.Sx") for calculations
  safe_labels <- make.names(corrplotlist)
  
  # 2. Create a lookup table to map safe labels back to pretty labels
  label_lookup <- setNames(corrplotlist, safe_labels)
  # ===============================================================================

  # Prepare the data by renaming columns to the SAFE labels
  original_vars <- names(corrplotlist)
  plot_data <- df[, c("id", original_vars)]
  names(plot_data)[match(original_vars, names(plot_data))] <- safe_labels # Use safe_labels here

  # Call the function with the safe labels for calculation AND the lookup table for the final plot
  correlation_heatmap <- generate_correlation_heatmap(
    data = plot_data,
    daily_items_raw = safe_labels, # Use safe_labels here
    id_var = "id",
    final_labels = label_lookup # Pass the lookup table for final relabeling
  )

  print(correlation_heatmap)

  # Define the final filename, including today's date
dated_output_file <- file.path(output_folder, 
                               paste0("Fig1_corr_", 
                                      format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), 
                                      ".png"))

# Save the plot directly to the final, dated file
save_png(plot = correlation_heatmap,
         filename = paste0("Fig1_corr_", 
                          format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), 
                          ".png"),
         folder = descriptives_folder,
         width = 11,
         height = 8.5)

} else {
  
  stop("Error: 'df' or 'corrplotlist' not found in the environment.")
}
```
